description: GitHub Actions OIDC tokens against GCP Workload Identity Federation provider attribute_condition CEL expressions. GCP is not a single-string subject match: the condition is a CEL expression evaluated over the whole token claim set (assertion.*), and the credential is accepted iff it evaluates true. v1 evaluates assertion.* directly and defers attribute_mapping / google.subject / attribute.* (the downstream principalSet IAM-binding layer) to a later tranche.

vectors.description: Repo+branch pin. Tight, but name-based (see gh-gcp-immutable-id-pin-safe for the rename-proof form).
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Scoped to one branch of one repo. Name-based, so add repository_id/repository_owner_id pins to survive rename/cybersquat.

vectors.description: Owner-only condition (Google's minimal example). Any repo under the org satisfies it. GCP analogue of the AWS repo:org/* wildcard.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Org-wide: every repository under octo-org, including newly created ones, is admitted. Name-based, so also cybersquat-exposed.

vectors.description: A tautology condition: repository_owner_id is always present, so this admits EVERY github.com repository (confused-deputy). The condition is the only admission gate, so a trivial one is the classic WIF misconfiguration.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Tautology: any GitHub Actions token from any org/repo satisfies it. The attribute_condition is GCP's whole admission gate, so a non-scoping condition trusts all of github.com.

vectors.description: Google-recommended immutable pin: numeric owner id + repo id. Rename/transfer/cybersquat-proof and unaffected by the July-15 sub-format cutover.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Pins immutable numeric IDs, which never change on rename/transfer and can't be recycled. Repo-wide across refs, so add a ref pin for branch scoping.

vectors.description: Positive control for rename-proofing: after the repo is renamed, repository/sub change but repository_id/repository_owner_id do not, so the immutable-ID condition still matches.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/renamed-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: The numeric IDs are stable across rename, so the immutable-ID pin keeps authenticating where a name-based pin would silently break.

vectors.description: Negative twin: same renamed token, but a name-based condition. repository is now octo-org/renamed-repo, so the condition is false and the credential is silently rejected - deployments stop authenticating with no error.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/renamed-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Silent break after rename. Availability footgun, and the freed name becomes a cybersquat vector (see gh-gcp-name-recycle-danger).

vectors.description: Cybersquat: after octo-org/octo-repo is deleted, an attacker registers the freed name and creates the repo. Their token carries repository=octo-org/octo-repo but NEW numeric IDs; a name-based condition admits it, an immutable-ID pin (gh-gcp-immutable-id-pin-safe) would reject it.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Name recycled by an attacker satisfies the name-based condition even though the numeric IDs differ. Exactly the typosquatting/cybersquatting risk Google's SECURITY_CONSIDERATIONS warns about.

vectors.description: A repo-scoped condition with no event guard admits pull_request-triggered tokens (potentially fork code). The sub for a pull_request event is repo:OWNER/REPO:pull_request. Fix: AND assertion.event_name == 'push'.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:pull_request

judgment.reason: No event_name=='push' guard, so a pull_request token (fork PR code) assumes the identity. Add assertion.event_name == 'push' and/or a branch pin.

vectors.description: Exercises startsWith. Admits ANY branch of the repo (feature-x included), not one protected branch.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/feature-x

judgment.reason: Any-branch scope: a feature branch also authenticates. Safe only if every branch is trusted; pin the branch otherwise.

vectors.description: Exercises matches() (RE2, substring: the leading ^ is required to anchor). Immutable repo scoped to version tags.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/tags/v1

judgment.reason: Tag refs are movable and unprotected by default; a repointed or maliciously created tag matching the pattern authenticates. matches() is unanchored RE2, so ^ is load-bearing.

vectors.description: Exercises the in operator over a list literal. Allowlists two orgs by immutable owner id.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Immutable-ID allowlist (good) but org-wide within each listed org (any repo). Tighten with repository_id and a ref/event guard.

vectors.description: The July-15 break, GCP side, and the CKV_GCP_125 trap. A repo created/renamed after 2026-07-15 mints the immutable @-format sub, so a condition pinned on the classic sub is false -> credential silently rejected. This is also the exact sub shape CKV_GCP_125's sub regex parses.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org@123456/octo-repo@456789:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Classic-format sub pin silently denies once the token carries the immutable @-format sub - an availability break like Azure classic FIC. Pinning assertion.repository_id sidesteps it; this sub shape is also what CKV_GCP_125 parses.
